steps.title: Convert the percent decrease to a decay factor
steps.content: Use two easy table points. At $x=0$, enrollment is $3{,}000$; after one year, it is $3000(0.98)=2940$. Enter $x_1=[0,1]$, $y_1=[3000,2940]$, and $y_1\sim a b^{x_1}$ in Desmos.

steps.title: Build the exponential model
steps.content: Desmos gives $a=3000$ and $b=0.98$, so the model is $f(x)=3000(0.98)^x$. The coefficient is the initial value, and the base is the yearly multiplier.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $f(x)=0.02 \cdot (3{,}000)^x$ — uses 3,000 as the base, which would grow enormously. Also 0.02 is the decay rate, not the initial value. Eliminated.
  • B) $f(x)=0.98 \cdot (3{,}000)^x$ — again uses 3,000 as a base, causing huge growth instead of small decline. Eliminated.
  • C) $f(x)=3{,}000 \cdot (0.02)^x$ — base 0.02 means 98% decrease per year, not 2%. Eliminated.
  • D) $f(x)=3{,}000 \cdot (0.98)^x$ — starts at 3,000 and decays by 2% each year.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
